scope: Book of Needs and related liturgical-resource access. This governs display/access classification before further Book of Needs or liturgical-service material is added.
coreDecision: Book of Needs and liturgical-resource access is role-aware, not merely role-restrictive. Default Book of Needs access remains lay-devotional and pastorally accessible, while role-aware access also provides appropriate and useful resources for those who serve in ordained or appointed liturgical roles.

firstPublicIntakeSplash.reason: Role discernment must not appear on the first public intake splash. The first entry flow remains tradition/family orientation and ordinary app entry.
firstPublicIntakeSplash.notes: Default lay-accessible Book of Needs is the normal experience, not a diminished mode. Do not frame lay devotional use as 'just' anything.

allowedWhen: inside Book of Needs settings where access tier matters
inside profile/defaults where the user chooses liturgical role access
when a user attempts to open a role-restricted liturgical-resource area
notAllowedWhen: general app entry before tradition/family selection
roleDiscernment.easternChristianRolePrompt: For Eastern Christian use, are you ordained or appointed to a liturgical order?

roleAccessPrinciples: Role access is order-specific. Reader, subdeacon, deacon, priest, and bishop access are not interchangeable.
Subdeacon access must not unlock deaconal, priestly, or episcopal material merely because the user is not a layperson.

Deacon access must not unlock priestly absolutions, priestly blessings, priestly silent prayers, episcopal material, or other texts proper to a higher order except where explicitly marked for reference use.
Priestly material must not be presented as ordinary lay prayer.
Episcopal material must not be presented as priestly or general clergy material.
Research/reference access may expose material for study without implying permission or appropriateness for liturgical performance.

purpose.protective: Prevent clergy-proper, sacramental, absolutional, exorcistic, ordination-related, blessing-specific, or otherwise order-proper texts from being presented as ordinary lay prayers.
purpose.usefulness: Provide actually appropriate and useful resources for users who serve in liturgical orders, including silent or secret prayers, preparatory prayers, vesting prayers, deaconal/subdeaconal/reader material, and service-related texts where source-governed.

defaultBookOfNeeds.description: The default public Book of Needs experience is for ordinary Christian devotional needs: repentance, thanksgiving, sickness, travel, work, family, fear, grief, preparation, and asking God's help.

futureIngestionRequirements: Before importing from the Big Book of Needs or similar Eastern service books, classify candidate texts by title, source, tradition, need category, access tier, default lay visibility, reference-only posture, and reason.
Do not dump priestly, episcopal, sacramental, exorcistic, absolutional, or blessing-specific material into the default Book of Needs dropdown.
Lay-visible batches should prioritize devotional needs before service-only material.
Role-specific batches should provide useful resources proper to that order rather than merely hiding material from lay users.
Silent or secret prayers may be included when source-governed and role-appropriate, but must not be surfaced as general lay devotional prayers.
relatedDecision: Some Eastern Book of Needs/Euchologion material is not appropriate for lay self-use. The app may include such material in the corpus, but display must be role-aware and tradition-honest.
